decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to control the board with efficient creatures like Guardian of the Guildpact and Outlaw Medic, while also using Prismatic Strands and Malevolent Rumble to protect them. The deck also aims to gain life and generate card advantage with cards like Armadillo Cloak and Raffine's Informant. Open the Gates allows for easy mana fixing and ramp, while the Gates package of lands provide a variety of mana symbols for casting spells. Overall, the deck focuses on controlling the game and out-valuing the opponent.

The deck revolves around the combo of using Tireless Tribe in combination with various spells to pump its power and toughness, then using Inside Out to switch its power and toughness, allowing for huge bursts of damage to be dealt to the opponent in a single turn. The deck also includes cards to protect the combo pieces and ensure consistency in drawing and finding the cards needed to execute the combo efficiently. The goal is to quickly assemble the combo and overwhelm the opponent with a sudden and unexpected burst of damage.

The main concept of this Gruul Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and efficient mana ramp spells to quickly build a powerful board presence. By utilizing cards like Arbor Elf, Utopia Sprawl, and Eldrazi Repurposer, the deck aims to accelerate its mana production and deploy threats like Annoyed Altisaur and Avenging Hunter quickly. Writhing Chrysalis and Malevolent Rumble provide additional removal and disruption to keep the pressure on the opponent. Overall, the deck focuses on applying early pressure and maintaining a strong board presence to secure a quick victory.

This deck focuses on assembling the Urzatron lands (Urza's Mine, Urza's Power Plant, Urza's Tower) to generate a large amount of mana quickly. The deck utilizes control elements such as counterspells and removal spells to disrupt the opponent's game plan while deploying powerful creatures like Maelstrom Colossus and Ulamog's Crusher to close out the game. The deck also includes card draw and tutoring effects to ensure consistency in finding key pieces. The goal is to overpower the opponent with big creatures and overwhelming mana advantage.

The main focus of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt the opponent through counter spells and removal, while also maintaining card advantage through card draw and recursion. The deck includes a mix of creatures with ETB effects, such as Augur of Bolas and Faerie Seer, along with powerful counter spells like Counterspell and Spellstutter Sprite. Removal spells like Snuff Out and Cast Down help control the board, while cards like Brainstorm and Lórien Revealed provide card selection and advantage. The deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent in a grindy control matchup.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of counterspells, removal, and card advantage. It utilizes Ephemerate to generate value from enter-the-battlefield effects of creatures like Mulldrifter and Archaeomancer. The deck also includes card draw and selection spells like Preordain and Augur of Bolas to ensure consistency. The inclusion of a diverse array of instant-speed answers like Skred and Lightning Bolt allows the deck to disrupt opponent's game plan efficiently. By efficiently managing resources and leveraging powerful synergies, this deck aims to outlast and outmaneuver opponents in the long game.

This deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with a fast and aggressive playstyle, combining creatures that deal damage when spells are cast with direct burn spells. The strategy is to quickly reduce the opponent's life total by combining creature damage and burn spells, while also utilizing card draw effects to maintain a consistent flow of threats and resources. The deck is designed to be fast and efficient, aiming to win the game before the opponent has a chance to stabilize.

The main idea of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive playstyle. Utilizing creatures like Burning-Tree Emissary, Goblin Bushwhacker, and Voldaren Epicure, as well as burn spells such as Galvanic Blast and Lightning Bolt, the deck aims to deal damage quickly and efficiently. Support cards like Rally at the Hornburg and Experimental Synthesizer help maintain pressure on the opponent. With a focus on speed and aggression, this Red Deck Wins archetype aims to win the game before the opponent has a chance to stabilize.

This deck focuses on using Gates to control the board and disrupt opponents while utilizing various spells and creatures to gain card advantage and protect key permanents. With a mix of removal, card draw, counter magic, and creatures with protection, the deck aims to slow down the opponent's game plan while establishing a strong board presence. The key strategy is to efficiently control the flow of the game through a combination of versatile spells and resilient creatures, ultimately outlasting the opponent and securing victory through attrition.

This deck focuses on quickly building a board of Goblin creatures and utilizing their abilities to create a powerful combo that can deal a large amount of damage to the opponent. By casting low-cost Goblins and using cards like Goblin Bushwhacker and Goblin Grenade, the deck can surprise the opponent with a burst of damage. With efficient removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Fireblast as backup, the deck aims to overwhelm and defeat the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize.

The main focus of this Pauper Aggro - Hot Dogs deck is to quickly pump up and empower creatures like Crackling Cyclops and Kiln Fiend with low-cost spells like Mutagenic Growth and Lightning Bolt, allowing them to deal massive damage early on. Festival Crasher and Ghitu Amplifier also contribute to the aggressive strategy. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a flurry of spells and combat tricks, utilizing cards like Temur Battle Rage and Ancestral Anger to push through for lethal damage. The inclusion of cantrips like Lava Dart and Crash Through ensures consistent card draw and selection.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and direct burn damage spells. By efficiently using cards like Guttersnipe and Masked Meower to deal damage, along with spells like Lightning Bolt and Fireblast, the deck aims to quickly reduce the opponent's life total to zero. Additionally, cards like Grab the Prize and Highway Robbery help to maintain card advantage and provide additional resources to keep up the pressure. This deck aims to win the game quickly through a combination of creature damage and spell burn.

The Mono Blue Aggro deck focuses on quickly playing low-cost aggressive creatures like Delver of Secrets and Cryptic Serpent to deal damage to the opponent efficiently. The deck then uses cheap counter spells and bounce effects like Boomerang to control the board and protect its creatures. The deck also includes card draw and self-mill cards like Ponder and Thought Scour to maintain card advantage and fuel the graveyard for spells like Tolarian Terror. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with fast, aggressive plays while controlling the game tempo.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly establish a board presence with aggressive creatures like Avenging Hunter and Nyxborn Hydra. Cards like Arbor Elf and Utopia Sprawl help ramp up mana production to play bigger threats like Bannerhide Krushok. Writhing Chrysalis and Jewel Thief provide additional value and disruption for the opponent. Malevolent Rumble can help clear the way for your creatures to attack, while Ram Through can deal direct damage to the opponent's creatures. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and powerful mono green aggro strategy.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game through countering spells and removing threats with burn spells, while also generating value with cards like Murmuring Mystic and Lórien Revealed. The deck aims to maintain board control and slow down the opponent's game plan, eventually overwhelming them with card advantage and efficient removal. The inclusion of snow-covered lands enables the use of Skred as a potent removal spell, while also providing additional synergy with cards like Tolarian Terror. Overall, this UR Control deck focuses on disruption, card advantage, and efficient removal to secure a win.

The main concept of this deck is to flood the board with cheap white creatures to overwhelm the opponent quickly. By playing efficient creatures like Kor Skyfisher and Lunarch Veteran, combined with anthem effects like Guardians' Pledge and Battle Screech, the deck aims to build a fast and aggressive board presence. Support spells like Prismatic Strands and Journey to Nowhere help control the opponent's board while maintaining pressure. The deck also includes card draw options like Raffine's Informant and Militia Bugler to ensure a consistent stream of threats.
